title: Neon Nocturne
synopsis: A neon noir investigation source used to prove the world compiler can ingest grounded mystery worlds, not only anime, fantasy, skyport, botanical, or undersea settings. Night-shift investigators in a rain-soaked market must recover evidence, repair a signal lens, and expose a forged confession before the precinct closes the case.

locations.name: Rain Market
locations.description: A wet plaza of noodle steam, flickering signs, and witness stalls.

locations.name: Signal Booth
locations.description: A narrow city booth where broken lenses scatter police-band light.

locations.name: Rooftop Laundry
locations.description: Clotheslines and apartment ladders above the market noise.

locations.name: Evidence Desk
locations.description: A precinct counter where chain-of-custody slips are checked.

locations.name: Floodlit Overpass
locations.description: A concrete overpass where forged witness marks were painted.

locations.name: Substation Alley
locations.description: A humming service lane where old transformers hide blackmail drops.

characters.name: Reva
characters.description: A careful runner trying to recover the badge that proves which officer touched the file.
goals: recover the witness badge before the desk closes
memories: The witness badge disappeared near the Signal Booth after midnight.

characters.name: Milo
characters.description: A precise tech who needs a glass signal lens to replay the police-band flash.
goals: recover the glass signal lens from the Substation Alley
memories: A signal lens rolled into the alley during the staged overpass alarm.

characters.name: Aya
characters.description: An advocate who needs physical proof before naming the forger.
goals: recover the blackmail tape from the overpass
memories: A blackmail tape was seen under the Floodlit Overpass.

characters.name: Sol
characters.description: A sleepless watcher who knows the market lights did not fail naturally.
memories: The market power stayed steady; the confession panic feels staged.

characters.name: Kade
characters.description: A polished fixer painting fake witness marks to force a quick confession.
goals: close the case before proof reaches the Evidence Desk
move the blackmail tape through Substation Alley
secrets: Kade painted the false witness marks under the Floodlit Overpass.
memories: Forged witness marks work best when the desk panics before checking the tape.

conflicts.title: A forged confession threatens the rain market case
conflicts.objective: Force the Night Desk to close the case before the evidence chain is repaired.
conflicts.clue: Pink neon paint on the false witness marks points back toward the Rain Market.

artifacts.name: Witness badge
artifacts.description: A brass desk badge proving which officer handled the file.
artifacts.clue: The badge slid beneath the signal switch.

artifacts.name: Signal lens
artifacts.description: A glass lens needed to replay the police-band flash.
artifacts.clue: The lens rolled away during the staged overpass alarm.

artifacts.name: Blackmail tape
artifacts.description: A magnetic tape with the real confession timing.
artifacts.clue: The tape is wet but still readable.

artifacts.name: Forged confession note
artifacts.description: A note timing the panic with the early case closure.
artifacts.clue: The note redirects blame away from the Glass Jackals.

artifacts.name: Desk radio
artifacts.description: A precinct radio used to confirm evidence-chain repairs.
artifacts.clue: The radio needs proof before closing the case.
